name: Apple and Rye Bread Pudding
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 cups Cubed fresh or stale sourdough, wheat, or rye bread
1 tbsp Caraway seeds OR Anise seeds
2 tbsp Mellow or sweet miso paste
2 cups Amazake beverage (original) OR Vanilla soy milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Cube the bread and toss it in a large bowl with raisins and caraway seeds. Stale bread can be used instead of fresh.
2. Toss the apples in a bowl with bread cubes.
3. Combine apple, sunflower butter or tahini, 1 cup water, miso paste, and amazake or soy milk in a separate bowl until well blended.
4. Pour the liquid mixture over the apples and bread. Stir to coat evenly.
5. Spoon the mixture into a lightly oiled 2-quart casserole dish or two 9-inch pie plates or cake pans. Cover with an oven-safe lid.
6. Allow the mixture to sit for 2 to 4 hours so the bread can soak up flavors.
7. Bake covered at 350°F for 1 hour, or until slightly firm and the mixture pulls away from the sides slightly.
8. Remove from the oven and spoon into individual dessert cups using an ice cream scoop. Allow to cool slightly before serving.
Total calories per serving: 242
Fat: 4 grams
